register.note: The new hero is full content-width and poster-legible — labelled scatter with hand vs machine rules and the 70%/97% contrast flanking it carries the whole thesis in one frame, matching Ciechanowski's iconic opening even if its dots-on-cream read slightly busier.

Like Seeing-Theory drawing the residual squares, the hero puts the mechanism on the canvas — both decision boundaries plus red-ringed misclassified points as the visible error, with the 70%/97% numbers quantifying the consequence in-graphic.

The See-it plot self-labels ('your rule', titled 'THE RULE YOU WRITE — ONE FEATURE, ONE CUT · 70%', axes) at Distill's bar, though the 'tops out short' claim still lives in adjacent prose rather than an on-graphic caption.

Distill-soft cream surface, restrained chrome, clean focal hierarchy; the prior opening dead air is resolved by the hero+masthead balance, with only a moderate lower-left void in the See-it column remaining.

Conservatively from statics, the motions are explanatory — the hero's single line-ease load, the 'Learn from the examples' boundary tilt, and the live Break-it slider — though steppability can't be fully verified from a frame.

Semantic grammar held cleanly like R2D3 — truth dots by class, error reserved for mislabel rings and the 64% true-population drop, accent/teal for the learned rule and live state, neutral param slider, AA mono labels, no all-red dilution.

hero.note: The doorway thesis as a specimen: hand rule (70%, mistakes ringed) vs machine rule (97%, eased in), accuracy contrast flanking. To push toward 4: heavier dot/ring weight at thumbnail scale and de-collide the 'machine's rule' label.

assessment.note: Item 05 is a genuine open transfer prompt (free-text résumé-screening case, commit-then-reveal) and item 04 is a hands-on lab task, so the Explain act is not an MCQ stack.

verdict.summary: The new doorway hero clears hero-as-protagonist and mechanism-in-the-picture at floor 3, every register dimension matches the benchmark, the prior opening dead air is resolved, and the transfer is open — advance. Push-to-4 polish: heavier hero dots, an in-canvas 'tops out' annotation, See-it lower-left dead air.
